channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Strategic Investment Solutions Inc.
item.description:
Strategic Investment Solutions Inc. reported a state registry affecting - records in ORLAND PARK, IL.
📊 Records Affected: - | 📍 Location: ORLAND PARK, IL | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday matches last year’s zero-day high with six actively exploited vulnerabilities
item.description:
Microsoft said three of the exploited vulnerabilities were publicly known, suggesting attackers already had details about the defects prior to Tuesday’s release. The post Microsoft Patch Tuesday matches last year’s zero-day high with six actively exploited vulnerabilities appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 0APT ransomware group rises swiftly with bluster, along with genuine threat of attack
item.description:
Most signs suggest the group is running a massive hoax by claiming hundreds of initial victims, but at least some of the threat 0APT poses is grounded in truth backed by proven capabilities. The post 0APT ransomware group rises swiftly with bluster, along with genuine threat of attack appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA to host industry feedback sessions on cyber incident reporting regulation
item.description:
One industry official told CyberScoop the town halls are probably not what CIRCIA needs right now. The post CISA to host industry feedback sessions on cyber incident reporting regulation appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google finds state-sponsored hackers use AI at ‘all stages’ of attack cycle
item.description:
The research underscores how AI tools have matured in their cyber offensive capabilities, even as it doesn’t reveal novel or paradigm shifting uses of the technology. The post Google finds state-sponsored hackers use AI at ‘all stages’ of attack cycle appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Apple discloses first actively exploited zero-day of 2026
item.description:
The vendor said the memory-corruption defect was exploited to target specific people, but it did not describe the objectives of the attack. The post Apple discloses first actively exploited zero-day of 2026 appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Proofpoint acquires Acuvity to tackle the security risks of agentic AI
item.description:
Proofpoint is snapping up the startup to solve the industry’s newest headache: knowing what your autonomous AI is actually doing. The post Proofpoint acquires Acuvity to tackle the security risks of agentic AI appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SolarWinds WHD Attacks Highlight Risks of Exposed Apps
item.description:
Organizations that have exposed their instances of Web Help Desk to the public Internet have inadvertently made them prime targets for attackers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Top Cyber Industry Defenses Spike CO2 Emissions
item.description:
Organizations can improve their climate footprints by optimizing two specific cybersecurity protections, without incurring added risks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Rising: Do We Know Enough About the Data Populating It?
item.description:
Organizations remain reluctant to address the fact that AI can dangerously expose business operations as well as personal data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How to Stay on Top of Future Threats With a Cutting-Edge SOC
item.description:
CISOs should focus on harnessing and securing AI and building new skills among their people. Vision and change management can transform security.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Senegalese Data Breaches Expose Lack of Security Maturity
item.description:
Green Blood Group steals personal records and biometric data of the West African nation's nearly 20 million residents.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SpecterOps Launches BloodHound Scentry to Accelerate the Practice of Identity Attack Path Management
item.description:
Drawing on years of adversary tradecraft, SpecterOps experts work alongside customers to analyze and eliminate attack paths, protect critical assets, and stay ahead of emerging threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Booz Allen Announces General Availability of Vellox Reverser to Automate Malware Defense
item.description:
The AI-powered product delivers expert-grade malware analysis and reverse engineering in minutes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ivanti EPMM Zero-Day Bugs Spark Exploit Frenzy — Again
item.description:
It's time to phase out the "patch and pray" approach, eliminate needless public interfaces, and enforce authentication controls, one expert says.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Agents 'Swarm,' Security Complexity Follows Suit
item.description:
As AI deployments scale and start to include packs of agents autonomously working in concert, organizations face a naturally amplified attack surface.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nation-State Hackers Put Defense Industrial Base Under Siege
item.description:
Espionage groups from China, Russia and other nations burned at least two dozen zero-days in edge devices in attempts to infiltrate defense contractors' networks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Under Pressure to Bolster Defenses for BYOVD Attacks
item.description:
Threat actors are exploiting security gaps to weaponize Windows drivers and terminate security processes in targeted networks, and there may be no easy fixes in sight.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dutch Carrier Odido Discloses Data Breach Impacting 6 Million
item.description:
Hackers stole personal information such as names, addresses, and phone numbers from a customer contact system. The post Dutch Carrier Odido Discloses Data Breach Impacting 6 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Over 300 Malicious Chrome Extensions Caught Leaking or Stealing User Data
item.description:
With more than 37 million combined downloads, the extensions expose users to tracking and personal information theft. The post Over 300 Malicious Chrome Extensions Caught Leaking or Stealing User Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google Patches First Actively Exploited Chrome Zero-Day of 2026
item.description:
A Chrome 145 update fixes CVE-2026-2441, a vulnerability that can likely be exploited for arbitrary code execution. The post Google Patches First Actively Exploited Chrome Zero-Day of 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Warns of ClickFix Attack Abusing DNS Lookups
item.description:
Attackers are using DNS requests to deliver a RAT named ModeloRAT to targeted users. The post Microsoft Warns of ClickFix Attack Abusing DNS Lookups appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Android 17 Beta Strengthens Secure-by-Default Design for Privacy and App Security
item.description:
The latest Android version continues to improve security and privacy, according to its developers. The post Android 17 Beta Strengthens Secure-by-Default Design for Privacy and App Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dior, Louis Vuitton, Tiffany Fined $25 Million in South Korea After Data Breaches
item.description:
Luxury brands were among the dozens of major companies whose Salesforce instances were targeted by Scattered LAPSUS$ Hunters. The post Dior, Louis Vuitton, Tiffany Fined $25 Million in South Korea After Data Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dismantling Defenses: Trump 2.0 Cyber Year in Review
item.description:
The Trump administration has pursued a staggering range of policy pivots this past year that threaten to weaken the nation’s ability and willingness to address a broad spectrum of technology challenges, from cybersecurity and privacy to countering disinformation, fraud and corruption. These shifts, along with the president’s efforts to restrict free speech and freedom of the press, have come at such a rapid clip that many readers probably aren’t even aware of them all.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
item.description:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ApolloMD Confirms 626,500 Patients Affected by May 2025 Ransomware Attack
item.description:
The extent of a May 2025 ransomware attack on the Georgia-based physician- and clinician-owned staffing and management group ApolloMD has […] The post ApolloMD Confirms 626,500 Patients Affected by May 2025 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Pinehurst Radiology Associates & Tallahassee Memorial HealthCare Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
item.description:
Pinehurst Radiology Associates has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit over a January 2025 data breach, and Tallahassee Memorial […] The post Pinehurst Radiology Associates & Tallahassee Memorial HealthCare Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Breaches Announced by MedRevenu & EyeCare Partners
item.description:
Data breaches have been confirmed by the revenue cycle management company MedRevenu Inland Physicians Hospitalist Services, and the Missouri-based eye […] The post Data Breaches Announced by MedRevenu & EyeCare Partners appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Healthcare Remains the Sector Most Targeted by Ransomware Groups as Attacks Increase 49% YOY
item.description:
A new record was set for ransomware attacks last year, with disclosed ransomware attacks increasing by 49% year-over-year to a […] The post Healthcare Remains the Sector Most Targeted by Ransomware Groups as Attacks Increase 49% YOY appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Texas Attorney General Launches Investigation into Conduent Business Services Data Breach
item.description:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has announced that his office has launched an investigation into the data breach at Conduent […] The post Texas Attorney General Launches Investigation into Conduent Business Services Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Emergency Medical Services Authority & Compassion Health Care Settle Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
Emergency Medical Services Authority in Oklahoma and Compassion Health Care in North Carolina were sued over cyberattacks and data breaches. […] The post Emergency Medical Services Authority & Compassion Health Care Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: 2025 Healthcare Data Breach Report
item.description:
More than 700 healthcare data breaches affecting 500 or more individuals are being reported to the Department of Health and […] The post 2025 Healthcare Data Breach Report appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Breach Settlements Agreed by Centrelake Medical Group & Des Moines Orthopaedic Surgeons
item.description:
Class action lawsuits over data breaches at Centrelake Medical Group and Des Moines Orthopaedic Surgeons have been resolved with settlements. […] The post Data Breach Settlements Agreed by Centrelake Medical Group & Des Moines Orthopaedic Surgeons appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Do your Staff need Training on HIPAA in Emergency Situations?
item.description:
Emergencies in healthcare are not limited to extreme weather, wildfires, or other natural disasters. Today’s most disruptive incidents are just […] The post Do your Staff need Training on HIPAA in Emergency Situations? appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: South Korea blames Coupang data breach on management failure, not sophisticated attack
item.description:
Heekyong Yang and Hyunjoo Jin report: South Korean officials blamed a massive data leak last year at Coupang on management failure, rather than a sophisticated cyberattack, and urged the e-commerce giant to fix vulnerabilities in its security systems. Announcing the first findings of a government-led probe, the Science Ministry said on Tuesday a former Coupang... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Attor­ney Gen­er­al Ken Pax­ton Demands Infor­ma­tion from Blue Cross Blue Shield of Texas and Con­duent as Part of Inves­ti­ga­tion into Largest Data Breach in U.S. History
item.description:
Their headline was, “Attor­ney Gen­er­al Ken Pax­ton Demands Infor­ma­tion from Blue Cross Blue Shield of Texas and Con­duent as Part of Inves­ti­ga­tion into Largest Data Breach in U.S. History,” but that seemed terribly wrong. Is Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton using AI as for his research? “Largest Data Breach in U.S. History?” Doesn’t he remember... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Dutch phone giant Odido says millions of customers affected by data breach
item.description:
Zack Whittaker reports: Dutch phone company Odido has confirmed a data breach affected millions of its customers. The company said in a statement Thursday that unidentified hackers gained access to its customer contact system and covertly downloaded reams of customer information. A spokesperson for Odido told local Dutch media that the breach affects more than 6.2 million customers, or... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: New “Kurd Hackers Forum” Focuses on Middle Eastern Data Breaches and Leaks
item.description:
Reza abasi notes that there is a new forum called the “Kurd Hacker Forum” that focuses on databreaches in Iran, Syria, and Turkey. The domain was registered January 28, 2026. The forum, which is on the clear net, looks like it has the same format as the classic BreachForums, with the same types of sections... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cyber Attacks on Schools Plateaued in 2025, but More Records Exposed
item.description:
Abby Sourwine reports: Ransomware attacks against schools and universities held relatively steady in 2025, but the scale of data exposure rose sharply, driven in part by third-party software vulnerabilities and a handful of outsized higher education breaches. According to U.K.-based technology research company Comparitech’s latest education ransomware roundup, ransomware gangs globally claimed 251 attacks on educational... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Guernsey medical practice sanctioned after cyber criminals access patient data through email account
item.description:
itv reports: Guernsey’s Data Protection Authority (ODPA) has sanctioned First Contact Health after it failed to implement sufficient security measures to prevent a phishing attack. The cybersecurity breach saw fraudsters successfully target an employee’s email account, gaining access to confidential health data at the medical practice. First Contact Health became aware and reported the data breach... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Nevada unveils new statewide data classification policy months after cyberattack
item.description:
Eric Neugeboren reports: Nevada’s IT agency has rolled out a new policy aimed at standardizing the privacy of state data, months after a massive cyberattack crippled certain systems for weeks. The policy announced Wednesday from the Governor’s Technology Office marks the first time the state will have clear-cut categories for data sensitivity. Officials said this will allow... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: He tried to extort the Dutch police. It didn’t work out well for him.
item.description:
He wanted something in return for returning files to the Dutch police. What he got in return was an arrest. A press release from Dutch police sums it up: On Thursday evening around 7:00 PM, police arrested a 40-year-old man from Ridderkerk on Prinses Beatrixstraat in Ridderkerk for computer hacking. Due to a police error,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Marietta also affected by BridgePay ransomware attack.
item.description:
Marietta, Georgia is one of numerous entities affected by the BridgePay ransomware attack. On February 13, the city posted the following notice on its website: The City of Marietta is currently unable to process certain online credit card payments due to a recent service disruption by one of the City’s payment gateway providers. We are... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Montana hospital restores phones as cyber-related network disruptions persist
item.description:
Dysruption Hub reports: Livingston HealthCare in Livingston, Montana, says its phone system has been restored after a cybersecurity incident disrupted communications and led the hospital to take some systems offline, but network disruptions continue as restoration work proceeds. The nonprofit hospital said Feb. 13 that a “potential cybersecurity incident” disrupted its phone systems and network and that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Thames Valley Chamber of Commerce
item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to WORLDLEAKS have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: WORLDLEAKS | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

🎭 Threat Actor: KILLSEC | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

Verification alert Listings attributed to INCRANSOM have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...

item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to NOVA have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: NOVA | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Atlas Air: MUSE INSECURE
item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to EVEREST have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: EVEREST | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Snail mail letters target Trezor and Ledger users in crypto-theft attacks
item.description:
Threat actors are sending physical letters pretending to be from Trezor and Ledger, makers of cryptocurrency hardware wallets, to trick users into submitting recovery phrases in crypto theft attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: One threat actor responsible for 83% of recent Ivanti RCE attacks
item.description:
Threat intelligence observations show that a single threat actor is responsible for most of the active exploitation of two critical vulnerabilities in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM), tracked as CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Pastebin comments push ClickFix JavaScript attack to hijack crypto swaps
item.description:
Threat actors are abusing Pastebin comments to distribute a new ClickFix-style attack that tricks cryptocurrency users into executing malicious JavaScript in their browser, allowing attackers to hijack Bitcoin swap transactions and redirect funds to attacker-controlled wallets. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New ClickFix attack abuses nslookup to retrieve PowerShell payload via DNS
item.description:
Threat actors are now abusing DNS queries as part of ClickFix social engineering attacks to deliver malware, making this the first known use of DNS as a channel in these campaigns. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Canada Goose investigating as hackers leak 600K customer records
item.description:
ShinyHunters, a well-known data extortion group, claims to have stolen more than 600,000 Canada Goose customer records containing personal and payment-related data. Canada Goose told BleepingComputer the dataset appears to relate to past customer transactions and that it has not found evidence of a breach of its own systems. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Google patches first Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks this year
item.description:
Google has released emergency updates to fix a high-severity Chrome vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks, marking the first such security flaw patched since the start of the year. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Man arrested for demanding reward after accidental police data leak
item.description:
Dutch authorities arrested a 40-year-old man after he downloaded confidential documents that had been mistakenly shared by the police and refused to delete them unless he received "something in return." [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Washington Hotel in Japan discloses ransomware infection incident
item.description:
The Washington Hotel brand in Japan has announced that that its servers were compromised in a ransomware attack, exposing various business data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

Wakefield & Associates, LLC reported a data breach in California.
📍 Location: California | 📰 Source: California AG | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

Conduent Business Services, LLC reported a data breach in California.
